questions.question: For a floating body to be in stable equilibrium, which of the following conditions must be met?

options.text: The center of gravity is above the center of buoyancy.
The metacenter is above the center of gravity.
The buoyant force is greater than the weight of the body.
The density of the body is less than the density of the fluid.

questions.explanation: For a floating body to be stable, its metacenter (M) must be located above its center of gravity (G). This ensures that any small tilt creates a restoring moment that returns the body to its original position.

questions.question: When a container filled with liquid is accelerated horizontally with a constant acceleration 'ax', what is the resulting shape of the free surface of the liquid?

options.text: It becomes inclined at a constant angle to the horizontal.
It becomes curved, with the highest point at the front of the acceleration.

questions.explanation: In a horizontally accelerating container, the free surface of the liquid tilts at a constant angle (tan θ = -ax/g). This inclination is due to the pressure gradient created by the horizontal acceleration.

questions.question: What is the characteristic shape of the free surface of a liquid when a cylindrical container filled with it is rotated about its central vertical axis at a constant angular velocity 'ω'?
questions.explanation: When a fluid in a cylindrical container rotates at a constant angular velocity, the free surface takes the shape of a paraboloid of revolution. This shape results from the balance between pressure, gravity, and centrifugal forces.
